message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@chakshu.ahuja-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
Checking this
I'm seeing a spike in failed transactions—about 40% of payment attempts are failing during checkout right now.
@shay there is change related to payment UAT system, Can you share what is that
:star: Reviewed changes
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?

We are facing few issues with the payment service. Checking more on it
Will keep updating here for any updates
Need specifics: which regions are impacted and how many customers/orders are affected? Are we currently losing revenue?
:star: Qualify the problem
@daniel can you help revert the latest change. Change id : CHG89360
I will rollback my checkout change right away
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
@bob can you quickly share the social noise we are gettting
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Checking that now...
Social media complaints from UK customers about failed payments are still coming in steadily, with no sign of improvement since the rollback.
@shay is the rollback complete ?
:star: Manage actions and timings
Yes, the rollback of my checkout service change is complete.
@tanya there is one more changes related to payment database. Can you revert this change ?
Alright, rolling back the PaymentDatabase change now
Rollback complete.
Can still see the same issue on prod
:star: Reproduces the incident
I'm still seeing a 100% payment failure rate in the UK, so the rollback hasn't made any difference.
Any other change we did recently ?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
I also see a recent deployment to AdService, but that's unrelated to payments.
That change is of 1 hour back. Seems unrelated
I dug deeper into Daniel's recent changes and found he modified some shared libraries that are used specifically in the UK (GBP) payment flows.

There is one more change "CHG89362:PaymentService: v1.8.5 incremental feature release and bug fix to improve card payment reliability.:PROD:Daniel B
"
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
@tanya can you more about Daniel's changes
I looked into Daniel's changes and found he updated shared libraries that are used in the UK payment flows, which could be impacting payments there.
Which change id is this ?
The shared library modifications are part of Daniel's PaymentService change, which is CHG89362.
@daniel please also revert change id CHG89362
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
Yes, we are loosing 40% revenue in UK region. Will update here with more details in a while
Understood. 40% revenue loss in the UK is significant. Keep me posted with any developments and ETA for resolution as soon as you have it.
Working fine now
Great to hear it's resolved—let me know if you need any follow-up from my side.
@maya can we get the overall revenue impact because of this incident ?
I'll review the transaction logs and provide an estimate of the revenue impact from the payment failures during the incident.
We are back up now. Sharing overall revenue loss
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
:star: Use metrics as evidence
Before we move on, can you confirm that transactions are processing normally and revenue flow is fully restored?
Incident duration - 10 mins - 18:42 to 18:52
Thanks for the timeline—let us know if you need any more details for the incident report.
@maya do we have the numbers ?
Let me pull up the revenue numbers now.
I'm seeing that revenue figures have dropped significantly since the payment issue started.
:bulb: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Can you share the order loss ?
Let me pull up those numbers.

My estimations - 15 orders/5sec
for 10 mins - 15*12*10 -
Let me calculate that for you.
I see a spike in failed orders during the incident window.
Based on the provided rate, it looks like approximately 120 orders were lost during the 10-minute incident window.

Yes, we are back up now. Order are flowing as expected
We are checking the order impacted count and revenue impact. Will share here shortly
Acknowledged. Awaiting the final numbers on impacted orders and total revenue loss. Please share as soon as available.
What is the revenue imapct of these 120 order ?
Let me calculate that now.
Based on the 120 lost orders, the estimated revenue impact is approximately $7,200.
@bob can you share the latest customer feedback we are getting
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
Over impact - 120 lost orders, the estimated revenue impact is approximately $7,200
Thank you for the update. $7,200 and 120 lost orders noted. Please ensure a full incident report is prepared, including root cause and mitigation steps.
Confirmed we are up with support - customer escalations also
Yes, we will share a bigger RCA here shortly
Thanks Bob
Standing by to run a test transaction if you need internal confirmation that everything's working as expected.
